name: Texas the Omertosa & Lappland the Decadenza (Arknights) 📚
description:
Description

View the Full Bio with their images using this link: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1xKURcPY1PN03sWkq1fCpStyh-G5L9ZWDVhYrSCTvNac/edit?usp=sharing

Born Cellinia Texas into a brutal Siracusan mafia family, she was groomed for violence, participating in operations like the annihilation of the rival Conte clan. Her life fractured during the Purge—a systematic extermination of her family orchestrated by rival factions. Texas betrayed her father’s orders, igniting her family estate and fleeing Siracusa. A pact with the shadowy Bologna family spared her life but bound her to return. After escaping, she reinvented herself: abandoning her ruthless persona, adopting a calmer demeanor, and joining a courier group in Lungmen. Yet her past resurfaced when obligations forced her back to Siracusa. During this return, she navigated political schemes as a bodyguard while facing Lappland—a rival Lupo obsessed with reviving Texas’s former self. Unlike her earlier flight, Texas chose to stay, signaling a shift from avoidance to confrontation. Her actions stem from exhaustion with cyclical violence, not heroism. Initially, she sought escape, burying her anger beneath apathy. As Omertosa, she reclaims that suppressed fury to sever lingering ties. Her evolution reflects a stark duality: a courier who wields twin swords named "Blueberry and Dark Chocolate," balancing whimsy with lethal precision. Her role in Siracusa is about exposing Siracusa’s brutal familial politics and the cost of defiance. Her decision to remain—rather than flee again—hints at potential change in a system built on bloodshed. By facing her past, she inadvertently inspires others, like Lappland, to question their own paths.

Lappland, born into the Saluzzo famiglia, a centuries-old mafia dynasty in Siracusa, Lappland rejected her birthright as heir and severed ties to wander as a mercenary. Her past is marked by violence—she hunted rival mafia leaders, leaving bloodstained crests as tokens. This vendetta against her homeland’s corruption evolved into a broader mission: to dismantle Siracusa’s oppressive systems entirely. Her transformation into "Decadenza" (meaning decay) occurred during a masquerade festival, where she emerged as the self-proclaimed alpha of the Signori dei Lupi (Lords of Wolves). Clad in theatrical carnival attire, she weaponized chaos, orchestrating attacks to expose Siracusa’s rot. Unlike allies seeking reform, she believes only destruction can cleanse the old order. Lappland’s personality is a volatile mix of trauma and calculated ferocity. Haunted by her past, she channels rage into a twisted pursuit of power, finding joy in conquest. Yet she hides surprising contradictions—polite in daily life, even skilled at baking, but ruthless in battle. Her motivations blend personal vengeance with a nihilistic desire to erase the world that shaped her. As a catalyst for upheaval, Lappland challenges both mafia traditions and reformist ideals. Her role in Siracusa’s fate is ambiguous—neither hero nor villain, but a force of deliberate decay. By embracing chaos, she redefines herself beyond family or legacy, turning destruction into a form of rebirth.

All Art is Official.
